name: Stack Basics
description: A stack follows LIFO order: the most recently added element leaves first.
exampleJava: Deque<Integer> st = new ArrayDeque<>(); st.push(10); int top = st.peek();
commonMistakes: Mixing FIFO queue behavior with LIFO stack behavior
Calling pop on an empty stack

name: Array-Based Stack
description: Use an array and a top pointer to implement push, pop, and peek in constant time.
commonMistakes: Forgetting overflow and underflow checks
Updating top in the wrong order

name: Push Pop Peek
description: Core stack operations should all run in O(1).
exampleJava: st.push(x); int y = st.pop(); int z = st.peek();
commonMistakes: Using remove(0) and shifting elements
Reading the top without checking emptiness

name: Balanced Parentheses
description: Push opening brackets and match them against closing brackets in order.
exampleJava: if (ch == '(') st.push(ch); else if (st.isEmpty() || st.pop() != '(') ok = false;
commonMistakes: Not checking stack emptiness before pop
Forgetting to verify the stack is empty at the end

name: Reverse Using Stack
description: Push items once, then pop them to reverse their order.
exampleJava: for (char ch : s.toCharArray()) st.push(ch); while (!st.isEmpty()) out.append(st.pop());
commonMistakes: Printing in the same order after pushing
Using a stack but still scanning backward directly

name: Postfix Evaluation
description: Push operands and apply operators to the top two operands when they appear.
exampleJava: int b = st.pop(); int a = st.pop(); st.push(a + b);
commonMistakes: Popping operands in the wrong order
Treating multi-digit parsing like single-character parsing

name: Min Stack
description: Track the current minimum alongside normal stack operations.
commonMistakes: Losing the previous minimum after pop
Recomputing the minimum with a full scan

name: Monotonic Stack
description: Keep the stack increasing or decreasing so each element is pushed and popped at most once.
commonMistakes: Using the wrong comparison direction
Forgetting whether the stack stores indices or values

name: Stock Span
description: Use a decreasing stack of indices to find how far the current value can extend left.
exampleJava: while (!st.isEmpty() && price[st.peek()] <= price[i]) st.pop(); span[i] = st.isEmpty() ? i + 1 : i - st.peek();
commonMistakes: Using values when indices are required
Getting the span formula off by one

name: Next Greater Element
description: Maintain a decreasing stack so the next larger value can be found in linear time.
exampleJava: while (!st.isEmpty() && st.peek() <= arr[i]) st.pop(); ans[i] = st.isEmpty() ? -1 : st.peek();
commonMistakes: Scanning to the right with a nested loop
Using the wrong direction for traversal

name: Previous Smaller Element
description: Keep an increasing stack so the nearest smaller element on the left can be found quickly.
commonMistakes: Not clearing larger elements before answering
Confusing smaller with greater

name: Expression Conversion
description: Use operator precedence and a stack to convert infix notation to postfix.
commonMistakes: Ignoring operator precedence
Forgetting to pop until '(' when handling ')'

name: Largest Rectangle in Histogram
description: Use a monotonic increasing stack to determine the widest rectangle for each height.
exampleJava: while (!st.isEmpty() && h[st.peek()] > h[i]) { int height = h[st.pop()]; ... }
commonMistakes: Using the wrong left boundary after popping
Forgetting the final cleanup after the scan

name: Stack Simulation
description: Simulate stack pushes and pops to validate sequences or collisions.
exampleJava: for (int x : pushed) { st.push(x); while (!st.isEmpty() && st.peek() == popped[j]) { st.pop(); j++; } }
commonMistakes: Trying to reason without simulating the actual stack state
Forgetting repeated pop opportunities after one push
